pundit.name: Alexi Lalas
pundit.take: **Let me tell you something, "The Noisemaker," you've got all the chaos of a teenager who picked three teams that didn't even qualify, yet you're tied for third—what does that say about the rest of you?!** Devin, look at me. LOOK AT ME. You're sitting pretty in first place with a whopping total of 4 points, thanks to your selection of Bosnia, who might as well be an imaginary team for all the good they’ve done you. How you snagged that top spot is more a comment on the abysmal performances of the other so-called "managers" than any brilliant strategy on your part. Gayden, "The Backpass Assassin," your cunning play of England and Japan has kept you afloat with Gayden-style calmness, but you're still not stepping up to the plate, buddy! And Gunner, "Bubba G," what happened to your French flair? A measly point in the standings is a crime against the fantasy World Cup gods! Oh, and "Mustard Boy" Zach, your midfield selections are a weak condiment on the hotdog of global soccer, my friend. Switzerland's draw with Qatar was your sole savior, barely inching you into the realm of relevancy with one sad little point. The standings right now are a reflection of the grit and toughness of 1994 U.S. soccer, and believe me, boys, most of you fall very short. The moral of the story? When you aim for the stars, at least don’t trip over the curb.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Brazil Pick Remains Spectator as Switzerland Settles for Draw
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy's strategy is as tangy as it is effective.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin's Japan Poised for Showdown with Dutch Masters
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden hopes Japanese precision can handle the Oranje creativity.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Undrafted Masters Set to Embarrass Ivory Coast and Backpass
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker banks on Ecuador making Gayden's day quieter.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G Clings to Hope as DR Congo Lurks at the Bottom
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner still waiting for his tier four miracle.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gayden's Korea Republic is his best and worst team? Guess it's all or nothing.
Rafe's Tier-1 Germany is his only lifeline; way to draft in the dark.
Rafe smacks down Devin's South Africa with a 3-point swing. Rookie's got moves!
The sim's as shaky as Gunner's chances; favorite until a kid showed up.
